check: 51 states present in all tables
question: Do we have all 51 states in every table?

check: No duplicate (state, claim_type, obs_date) keys
question: Is (state, claim_type, week) a unique key?

check: No nulls in key fields
question: Are key fields complete (no nulls)?

check: Values strictly positive, finite (parse consistency incl. int->float drift)
question: Is every value parseable, finite and positive?
threshold: 0 bad; startup-era zeros tolerated as WARN
note: all zeros sit in Jan-Jul 1986 — RULE: treat values <= 0 as missing before any log transform. FRED int->float format drift coerced cleanly. Position analysis in V1.4b.

check: Zero position: leading (ramp-in) vs interior (mid-sample dropout)
question: Are zeros ramp-in artifacts (leading) or mid-sample dropouts (interior)?
threshold: interior == 0 ideally; pre-1990 interior tolerated as WARN
note: leading zeros = series ramp-in, drop silently; INTERIOR zeros are dropouts inside live data (e.g. NJ iclaims Jul-1986: 13,777 -> 0 -> 0 -> 7,846) — must be masked BEFORE differencing or the surrounding WoW changes are corrupted too

check: Row counts comparable across states (current history)
question: Did any state's fetch silently truncate?
note: small raggedness at series start (states begin reporting a few weeks apart in 1986)

check: All obs_date are Saturdays (DOL week-ending)
question: Do obs dates follow the DOL Saturday week-ending convention?

check: Interior weekly gaps, pre-1990 startup era
question: Are there missing weeks in the pre-1990 history?
note: early DOL collection holes — RULE: forecasting pipelines must handle NaN weeks; do not forward-fill through gaps when computing WoW changes

check: Interior weekly gaps, post-1990 (live data issues)
question: Are there missing weeks in modern data (live issues)?
note: a recent missing week = state failed to report / FRED shows '.' — monitor for backfill; PIT features skip the week automatically (availability frontier)

check: Panel edges aligned across states
question: Do all states cover the same window?
note: common balanced window: 1986-02-08 -> 2026-06-13

check: ALFRED vintage boundary identified
question: How far back can point-in-time be reconstructed?
note: pre-boundary history (1986->2010-09) exists only as current values: usable for seasonality/climatology, NOT for backtesting

check: National CCNSA vintages land mid-week (Thu, or holiday-Wed)
question: Does the vintage calendar match the DOL mid-week release schedule?
note: Wednesday releases are the holiday-shifted reports (Thanksgiving, Jul 4, ...)

check: National CC first release = advance print (Sat + 12d = Thursday)
question: Is the national first release the advance print at obs+12d?
note: CC reference week trails the report by 12 days (one week behind initial claims)

check: State captures cluster on/next-day-after release days
question: On which weekday does FRED capture state data?
note: Mon captures are FRED ingestion delays, not release timing — see V3.5/V3.6

check: State first release vs national release date, same obs week
question: How long after the public release do state values appear?
metric: 32.6% within 0-2d of national release; 61.2% at +6-8d (next report)
threshold: >= 90% in one of the two report windows
note: IMPORTANT: this measures FRED/ALFRED CAPTURE order, not DOL publication order. DOL publishes state and national figures SIMULTANEOUSLY in the same Thursday report — states are not released later, they are ARCHIVED later by FRED. +6-8d cluster = pre-2020 (state series archived one report cycle later, storing the revised print); 0-2d cluster = 2020+ (archived the day after the release, storing the advance print). For backtests, ALFRED capture dates are therefore a CONSERVATIVE availability assumption.

check: Era gap pattern consistency (is +7d / +1d stable within each era?)
question: Is the state-vs-national gap pattern stable within each era?
metric: pre-2020: 97.8% at +6-8d; post-2020: 83.6% at +0-2d (+12.7% slip to +3-5d = Monday ingestion)
note: pre-2020 is rock-solid (median gap +7d every year). post-2020 is modal +1d (Friday) but NOT fully consistent: ~10% of captures slip to Monday (+4d), and the 2021 transition year is chaotic (~57% in-window). Backtest staleness post-2020: 13d typical, 16d conservative (Monday-slip tail). Negative gaps in the histogram are the 7 late-NATIONAL-archive weeks (V3.6) — not states being published early.

check: No state value available before its DOL report (leak check)
question: Could a backtest have seen state data before it was public?
metric: min state pub lag 13d across all 42k vintage rows
threshold: min lag >= 12d (nothing can precede the report that first contains it)
note: a NAIVE version of this check (state date < national ALFRED date) fires 357 false alarms on 7 weeks — those are weeks where the NATIONAL series was archived late (ALFRED artifact), not early state data — a negative gap never means the state number was PUBLIC first; see example below

check: Capture-lag regime stability
question: Has FRED's capture timing / stored print type been stable?
note: pre-switch ALFRED stores the REVISED print (captured Sat+19d, i.e. on the following report day); post-switch the ADVANCE print (captured Sat+13d, the day after release). Backtests MUST use per-era lags; the print content differs by era too.

check: Release dates weakly increasing in obs order (per state)
question: Do weeks become available in order?
note: an inversion means a later week became visible before an earlier one — harmless if tiny, but the PIT builder must use the cummax frontier (it does)

check: PIT panel look-ahead guard (cclaims_latest_obs released <= as_of_date)
question: Does the PIT panel contain any look-ahead?

check: Endpoint revision rate (first release vs current)
question: How often does a first print get revised at all?
note: NOT the near-zero rate a small-sample probe suggests: revisions are real but concentrated — see the by-state and by-year breakdowns; full exception list feeds V9.4

check: Revisions concentrated (chronic states + 2020-22 window)
question: Where are revisions concentrated?
metric: top-4 states = 56% of exceptions: Hawaii n=403 (med |0.2%|), Pennsylvania n=168 (med |0.1%|), New Mexico n=111 (med |3.0%|), Maryland n=104 (med |0.1%|)
note: chronic revisers publish systematically preliminary first prints — consider state-specific noise handling or a revised_later flag feature; 2020-22 corrections are pandemic-era admin fixes

check: Intermediate revisions bounded (full trajectory, 5 core states)
question: Do values change mid-life and net out (invisible to endpoint checks)?
metric: core states: 49 multi-valued obs of 10,552 (0.46%); Hawaii: 404 of 2151 (up to 5 values per obs)
note: core states are (near-)single-valued across all vintages, so V4.1's endpoint join captures their revision behavior; Hawaii revises repeatedly (up to 5 values per week) — its first prints are genuinely preliminary

check: Advance vs revised print: how different are they? (national calibration)
question: How big is the national advance-to-revised gap?
note: the typical advance->revised change is ~-0.1% (the advance slightly OVERSTATES claims), IQR +-0.3%, tails ~6% in COVID weeks. VERDICT: the V3.7 era mixing (revised prints pre-2020 vs advance prints post-2020) is a second-order inconsistency for state features — acceptable; add an era dummy if precise levels matter.

check: Stale-value runs (identical consecutive weeks, value >= 10k)
question: Are there frozen/stale stretches masquerading as data?
metric: 75 runs in current values; 34 in first-release values
note: known: CA late-2020 processing pause (EDD backlog) froze FIRST-RELEASE prints — visible in the vintage data even where current data was later corrected; treat runs as stale/missing, not signal

check: Extreme weekly moves classified (|robust z| > 6 on dlog)
question: Which extreme moves are real events vs calendar mechanics vs unexplained?
metric: 1477 extreme moves: 224 COVID, 732 holiday, 167 pre-1990 era, 354 unexplained modern
note: COVID moves are genuine macro events — keep but regime-flag; pre-1990 spikes reflect the noisier startup era (see V1.4/V2.2a); the modern unexplained list needs eyeballing before use

check: Rounded-reporting states identified (share of values % 100 == 0)
question: Do some states report rounded numbers (noise floor)?
note: rounded reporting inflates the noise floor for those states — matters for WoW features

check: TX 2024-06-01 first-print error confirmed in-dataset (48,748 -> 148,748)
question: Did the TX 2024-06 +100k first-print error really happen, and which print was wrong?
note: first release was the data error (dropped leading digit); current value is credible. A strict PIT feature on ~2024-06-14 used the erroneous print — add a data-error dummy or winsorize WoW changes in backtests.

check: Sum(51 states) reconciles with national CCNSA
question: Do the 51 states add up to the national total?
note: gap = PR/VI + timing; large transient deviations flag data problems in specific weeks

check: Cross-state co-movement (mean pairwise corr of YoY changes)
question: Does every state co-move with the national cycle?
note: a state decorrelated from the national cycle is a data-quality suspect, not an alpha source

check: Annual seasonality present and strong (ACF of dlog at lag 52, 2011-2019)
question: How strong is the annual seasonality to handle?
threshold: documented — NSA data requires seasonal handling
note: use YoY transforms, 52-week differencing, or explicit week-of-year/holiday controls

check: Holiday-week effect quantified (mean dlog, holiday vs regular weeks)
question: Are holiday weeks mechanically distorted?
note: if WARN: add holiday dummies or use 4-week MA features (pit already includes 4wma)

check: Transform recommendation validated (ADF: log level vs YoY log-diff)
question: Which transform makes the series stationary?
metric: California: lvl p=0.00, yoy p=0.000; Texas: lvl p=0.00, yoy p=0.000; New York: lvl p=0.00, yoy p=0.000; Florida: lvl p=0.07, yoy p=0.000; Michigan: lvl p=0.00, yoy p=0.000
threshold: YoY log-diff stationary (p < 0.05) for all sample states
note: work in YoY log-diff (or dlog with seasonal controls); levels are non-stationary

check: Persistence plausible in every state (AR1 of log level)
question: Is persistence plausible in every state?
note: weekly insured-unemployment stocks are extremely persistent; a low-AR1 state = noisy/broken data

check: COVID program caveat documented
question: What did continued claims exclude during COVID?
note: regular-state CC understates total insured unemployment during 2020-21; level-based cross-state comparisons in that window are biased — prefer changes or regime-flag it

check: PIT features indexed by information time (Friday grid, plausible staleness)
question: Are features indexed by information time?
metric: all Fridays: True; median cclaims staleness 20d
threshold: Friday grid; staleness within release-calendar bounds

check: Orientation: claims lead unemployment with positive sign (5 sample states)
question: Do claims lead unemployment with the right sign (wiring check)?
metric: California: peak corr 0.89 at k=0m; Texas: peak corr 0.88 at k=0m; New York: peak corr 0.90 at k=0m; Florida: peak corr 0.87 at k=0m; Michigan: peak corr 0.83 at k=0m
threshold: >= 3/5 states peak at k >= 0 with corr > 0.3
note: validates sign/alignment wiring only — NOT the lead-lag analysis itself

check: Weekly->monthly aggregation convention documented
question: How must weekly claims map onto monthly reference periods?
metric: LAUS reference week = week containing the 12th
note: when pairing claims with LAUS monthly data, align claims weeks to the reference week, not calendar-month averages (calendar averaging leaks post-reference-week info into the month)

check: PIT coverage complete after ramp-in
question: From which date is the PIT panel backtest-complete?
note: usable backtest window starts 2010-11-05

check: Universe stability (balanced panel over common window)
question: Is the universe stable (no entry/exit)?
note: 1-7 week differences = the interior gaps enumerated in V2.2 (pre-1990 + VA 2026)

check: Era-specific information lag documented for backtests
question: What staleness must a backtest assume, per era?
note: apply per-era staleness — a constant-lag assumption misprices the pre-2020 era by ~1 week

check: PIT rows carrying later-revised first prints (two tiers)
question: How many feature rows carry later-corrected values?
metric: t0 revised-later: 1386 rows (3.3%); any of t0..t7: 2823 rows (6.8%)
note: strict PIT correctly keeps these (that is what was known at the time) — but they are error-prone inputs concentrated in chronic-reviser states and 2020-22; RECOMMENDATION: add a revised_later flag column to the feature panel and winsorize WoW features

check: Manifest written (hashes, params, ranges)
question: Can the dataset be reproduced and audited byte-for-byte?
note: ALFRED queries with pinned realtime_* params are reproducible; re-fetch and diff hashes to audit
